summary: refactor!: replace TSE with pDFNet2+ PSE architecture
decisions: Replaced SpeakerBeam-SS TSE model with pDFNet2+ personalized speech enhancement
Implemented DeepFilterNet2 base with dual ERB+DF branch architecture
Added TinyECAPA for on-the-fly speaker similarity refinement
Switched from hard-clamp to sigmoid similarity gate for smoother conditioning
Made training conditioning causal by default to eliminate train/serve skew
Added epoch-aware PSEMixer with independent SNR/SIR scaling
Added TinyECAPA checkpoint loader with weights_only security
Added cosine-warmup LR scheduler to both training loops
tests: 78 tests passing covering models, data pipeline, training, losses, evaluation

summary: feat(scripts): add TOML-driven training and inference CLI
decisions: TOML config files as primary interface for reproducible experiments
tomllib (stdlib 3.11+) for zero-dependency parsing
--override key=value for quick parameter sweeps without editing config
Auto-detect train/val splits from dataset structure, fallback to speaker-disjoint random split
Model type auto-detection in inference via try-PDfNet2-then-DfNet fallback

summary: refactor(models): replace wespeaker dependency with native ECAPA-TDNN
decisions: Implemented native ECAPA-TDNN in separate ecapa_tdnn.py with exact WeSpeaker upstream layer names for checkpoint compatibility
Added dedicated compute_wespeaker_fbank_batch() with explicit hamming window instead of changing existing FBank defaults
Replaced lazy wespeaker import with native checkpoint loading supporting raw state_dict and wrapped formats
SE_Connect bottleneck uses channels//4 to match upstream for both c512 and c1024 variants
Removed unsafe TypeError fallback in torch.load since project requires torch>=2.4
checkpoint_path now required when backend not provided (no more implicit wespeaker fallback)
tests: 86 tests passing: +8 new tests for ECAPA forward shapes, checkpoint loading (raw/wrapped/projection/module prefix/frontend rejection/variant auto-detection)

summary: fix(models): adapt ECAPA-TDNN to SpeechBrain checkpoint format
decisions: Rewrote ecapa_tdnn.py to match SpeechBrain architecture: double-nested wrapper keys (conv.conv/norm.norm), channels*3 fusion, Conv1d SE, global-context ASP
ModelScope ECAPA checkpoint uses fundamentally different architecture from WeSpeaker — key remapping not viable due to dimension mismatches
Loader now targets SpeechBrain raw OrderedDict format, validates blocks.0.conv.conv.weight marker key
Added weights_only fallback for SpeechBrain OrderedDict compatibility

bugs.symptom: SpeakerEncoder fails to load ModelScope ECAPA-TDNN checkpoint
bugs.root_cause: Checkpoint uses SpeechBrain format with different layer names, fusion dims, and module structure vs WeSpeaker
bugs.fix: Rewrote ecapa_tdnn.py to match SpeechBrain architecture exactly

tests: 84 tests passing, real checkpoint loads successfully as [1, 192] embedding

summary: fix(data): prevent enrollment/target audio leakage and add gain/bandwidth augmentation
decisions: Target sampling reserves enrollment anchor via avoid_paths to guarantee disjoint files — eliminates need for fallback/relaxation logic
Added apply_random_gain (+-6dB) and apply_bandwidth_limit (sinc FIR lowpass 4-7kHz) to simulate recording level and channel variation
Augmentation applied to mixture only (not target label or enrollment) after mixing, before peak normalization
Pure PyTorch sinc+hamming FIR implementation — no scipy dependency

bugs.symptom: Enrollment could share audio files with target segment when speaker has few utterances and target concatenates multiple files
bugs.root_cause: avoid_paths only excluded target anchor path, not all files used in target concatenation
bugs.fix: _sample_speaker_segment_tracked returns all used paths; target sampling reserves enrollment anchor upfront

tests: 88 tests passing: +4 new tests for gain, bandwidth limit, path exclusion, short-speaker reservation

summary: fix(loss): align SpectralLoss with pDFNet2 paper defaults
decisions: Reverted under_suppression_weight from 2.0 to 1.0 — pDFNet2 paper (arxiv 2404.08022) and official DeepFilterNet loss.py both use symmetric weighting (factor_under=1)
Reverted SpectralLoss gamma from 0.6 to 1.0 — official DeepFilterNet SpectralLoss defaults to gamma=1.0
PDfNet2Loss gamma also changed 0.6→1.0, propagated to all sub-losses
The 2x asymmetry was identified as primary cause of conditioning collapse: model learned uniform -4dB attenuation instead of speaker-selective suppression

bugs.symptom: Model applies near-uniform -4.1dB attenuation regardless of speaker identity, ignoring conditioning
bugs.root_cause: under_suppression_weight=2.0 made leaving interference cheaper than risking target suppression, causing conditioning collapse
